purpose: Defines deterministic execution semantics for the strict Excel extraction in standards.json.

contract_binding.rule: A consumer must reject standards.json when metadata.contract.execution_spec.$ref or metadata.contract.execution_spec.version does not match this file.

rules.note: Use for nominal pipe size label filtering. Do not convert these labels directly to physical diameter unless an entry also gives nominal_mm.

standard_selection_order: filter by requested ot_code when provided
filter by requested type when provided
filter by requested system when provided
filter by requested radius when provided
filter by range_mm or range_inch when an applicable range exists
filter by exact entry nominal_mm, nominal_inch, diameter_pair_mm, or secondary_nominal_mm when provided
return ambiguity when more than one standard or entry remains

numeric_match.rule: Two numeric values match when abs(a - b) <= default_tolerance_mm.

missing_lookup.rule: Do not interpolate or derive missing lookup entries from neighboring rows.

ambiguous_lookup.rule: Caller must provide more selectors, such as ot_code, system, type, radius, nominal_mm, nominal_inch, or secondary_nominal_mm.

rounding.rule: Perform calculations with decimal arithmetic. Round only returned numeric results, not intermediate values.

resolution_order: select exact lookup entry when entries are present
apply formula tolerance only for fields governed by diameter_tolerance

lookup_table.matching: Match by standard_id and selected entry nominal_mm/secondary_nominal_mm. Do not interpolate.
lookup_table.formula: measured_diameter_mm must be between extracted min_diameter_mm and max_diameter_mm.
lookup_table.applies_when: Use when a standard has lookup rows but no diameter_tolerance classes in standards.json.

geometry_tolerance_fields.formula: min_value_mm = nominal_field_mm - value; max_value_mm = nominal_field_mm + value
geometry_tolerance_fields.note: This contract applies to extracted tolerance fields such as f_tolerance_mm. If a future source row proves a unilateral tolerance, store it as an explicit object instead of using the suffix convention.

diameter_tolerance_range.formula: limit_mm = max_diameter_mm - min_diameter_mm for the selected diameter tolerance rule

excluded.rule: Do not evaluate ovality for pass/fail; preserve source note for traceability.

requires_source_enrichment.rule: Do not evaluate ovality until the full per-entry source data is represented in standards.json.

lookup_table.matching: Match by standard_id and selected entry nominal_mm/secondary_nominal_mm. Do not interpolate.

ovality_tolerance.pass_fail_rule: roundness_ok = measured_ovality_mm <= limit_mm when a limit is evaluable; otherwise roundness_ok is null/not evaluated.

source_row.meaning: Original 1-based Excel row number in the sheet named by standard.sheet.

stable_ids.note: standards.json currently keeps strict source rows. Public API responses should add stable IDs without removing source_row.

strict_extraction_policy.rule: When the Excel source is ambiguous, preserve traceability and omit the ambiguous value rather than guessing.

errors.DIAMETER_OUT_OF_RANGE: Input diameter is outside the selected OT code range.
errors.NO_MATCHING_STANDARD: No standard matches the supplied selectors.
errors.NO_MATCHING_ENTRY: No lookup entry matches the supplied dimensions.
errors.AMBIGUOUS_STANDARD_OR_ENTRY: More than one standard or entry matches the supplied selectors.
errors.MISSING_TOLERANCE_CLASS: A formula standard requires an explicit diameter tolerance class.
errors.UNSUPPORTED_DERIVATION: Requested output requires interpolation, Excel formula reconstruction, or another derivation not allowed by the strict extraction policy.
